steps.title: Identify the monthly multiplier
steps.content: A $3\%$ increase means each month has $100\%+3\%=103\%$ of the previous month's total. As a decimal, that multiplier is $1.03$.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
The initial enrollment is $250$, and the multiplier is applied once for each month after March 2020. After $m$ months, the model is
$$
c=250(1.03)^m
$$

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content: Choice A uses only the percent increase as the base, Choice C uses a $30\%$ increase, and Choice D triples the enrollment each month. The model with starting value $250$ and multiplier $1.03$ is Choice B. The answer is B.
